passes.agent: unspecified — not provided in orchestrator dispatch record

findings.summary: AC-005 Postcondition 4 ingest-guard threshold off by the 8-byte HMAC tag — stated the router-side ingest guard rejects a raw datagram "shorter than 24 bytes", but the shipped wire layout ([8]HMACTag | [16]SVTNID | [8]NodeAddr | ...) and Decision 1/SEC-DW-01 both require 32 raw bytes (8-byte tag prefix + 24-byte key selector). A literal implementation would leave a 24-31-byte raw-datagram window that passes the length guard and then indexes out of bounds pre-authentication — CWE-770/400 class.
findings.remediation: story-writer — story v2.0→v2.1: AC-005 PC-4 corrected to "shorter than 32 bytes (8-byte HMAC tag + the 24-byte SVTNID/NodeAddr key selector) — equivalently, whose post-tag body is shorter than 24 bytes", plus an explanatory note on the body = post-tag-bytes convention and the 38-byte full-valid-frame minimum. Full-story sweep found AC-005 PC-4 was the sole occurrence. No AC added/removed (18 unchanged), points unchanged (8). input-hash unchanged (b4e0a5f, compute-input-hash --check confirmed no drift). STORY-INDEX v4.97→v4.98 (Backlog row + changelog row).

findings.summary: VP-080 carried stale ARCH-03 v1.7 citations (Property Statement + Source Contract) after ARCH-03 moved to v1.8 within the same elaboration burst (Ruling 3, added after VP-080 was minted). Semantically harmless — v1.8 only added the hop-2 relay-transport paragraph — but a version-pin hygiene break per POL-001. VP-080's stored input-hash was also stale independent of the citation fix, since one of its declared inputs (the rulings doc) grew from v1.2 to v1.3 after mint.
findings.remediation: architect — VP-080 v1.0→v1.1: both ARCH-03 citations re-pinned v1.7→v1.8; input-hash recomputed 82c4a75→c3d2fc5 via compute-input-hash --update (proactive, closed in the same pass rather than left for a later drift-detection finding). No property-statement, proof-method, or lifecycle content changed.

nitpicks_no_action.orchestrator_disposition: NO-ACTION — authoritative source already co-cited; version churn not warranted

passes.infrastructure_note: story-writer agent dropped mid-report after completing the fix (API connection closed) — work disk-verified complete by orchestrator, report reconstructed from disk; fifth mid-stream drop this cycle.
passes.notes: Both findings remediated same fix-burst (fix-burst 1). Fix-burst 1 close performed by state-manager, itself resumed once mid-task after its own stream drop (API connection closed) immediately following disk-state confirmation and before this JSON was written — no partial writes, resumed cleanly per orchestrator nudge.
